prerequisites.description: Required pre-nursing prerequisites (completed before admission to the nursing major; not counted toward nursing credits)
notes: General Chemistry I (e.g. CHEM101 or equivalent)
Human Development across the Lifespan (e.g. PSYC260 or equivalent)

electives.description: Liberal Arts and Science elective requirements — courses outside nursing to fulfill degree breadth
electives.notes: Any approved humanities, social science, or natural science course meeting SON breadth requirements

constraints: Admission to the BSN program requires a minimum GPA of 3.0 in prerequisite sciences.
NURS110 and NURS111 (A&P I and II) must be completed before NURS150 and NURS200.
NURS220 (Foundations of Nursing Practice) is the gateway course; must be completed before all 300-level NURS courses.
NURS210 must be completed before NURS300; NURS211 before NURS310.
NURS420 (Senior Practicum) requires completion of all 300-level clinical and theory courses.
Minimum grade of C required in all NURS courses; minimum grade of C+ required in all sciences.
Clinical placements (NURS221, 301, 311, 321, 331, 341, 351, 420) require current CPR certification, health clearance, and background check.
Total credits: approximately 120-128.

transfer_credit.description: Credit awarded for prior RN education and licensure
notes: Students holding an active RN license receive credit for all foundational and clinical nursing courses (NURS101 through NURS341).
Credit equivalencies determined upon evaluation of ADN or diploma transcripts.
Transfer credit typically accounts for 30-60 nursing credits.

electives.description: Liberal Arts and Science electives to complete the baccalaureate degree
electives.notes: Any approved humanities, social science, or natural science course meeting SON breadth requirements

constraints: Applicants must hold a current, unrestricted RN license in New Jersey or a compact state.
Minimum cumulative GPA of 2.5 required for admission.
Program designed to be completed fully online with flexible clinical hours arranged locally.
NURS400 (Nursing Research) and NURS410 (Nursing Leadership) are required new coursework for RN-BSN students.
NURS420 (Senior Practicum) is fulfilled through a supervised preceptored experience in the student's community.
Total new credits required: approximately 30-36 beyond transfer credit. Total degree credits: 120.

core_required.description: MSN core courses required for all specializations (18 credits)

specialization_required.description: Family Nurse Practitioner specialization courses (18 credits)

constraints: Total credits: 45 (core + specialization + elective).
MSN500, MSN501, and MSN502 (Advanced Assessment, Pathophysiology, Pharmacology) must be completed before MSN530.
MSN530 (Diagnostic Reasoning) must be completed before any FNP specialization courses.
FNP specialization courses must be taken in sequence (MSN540 → MSN541 → MSN542 → MSN543 → MSN544 → MSN545).
Minimum 500 supervised clinical hours required (distributed across FNP practicum courses).
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required to remain in good standing.
Applicants must hold an active RN license and a BSN from a CCNE- or ACEN-accredited program.
Clinical placements require active RN license, malpractice insurance, and health clearance.

electives.description: Choose one graduate nursing elective (3 credits)

notes: Graduates eligible to sit for AANP or ANCC Family Nurse Practitioner certification examination.

core_required.description: MSN core courses required for all specializations (18 credits)

specialization_required.description: Adult-Gerontology Primary Care NP specialization courses (12 credits)

electives.description: Choose two graduate nursing electives (6 credits)

constraints: Total credits: 45 (core + specialization + electives).
MSN500, MSN501, and MSN502 must be completed before MSN530.
MSN530 must be completed before any AGPC NP specialization courses.
AGPC NP courses must be taken in sequence (MSN550 → MSN551 → MSN552 → MSN553).
Minimum 500 supervised clinical hours required.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required to remain in good standing.
Applicants must hold an active RN license and a BSN from a CCNE- or ACEN-accredited program.
notes: Graduates eligible to sit for ANCC Adult-Gerontology Primary Care NP certification examination.

core_required.description: MSN core courses required for all specializations (18 credits)

specialization_required.description: Psychiatric-Mental Health NP specialization courses (12 credits)

electives.description: Choose two graduate nursing electives (6 credits)

constraints: Total credits: 45 (core + specialization + electives).
MSN500, MSN501, and MSN502 must be completed before MSN530.
MSN530 must be completed before any PMHNP specialization courses.
PMHNP courses must be taken in sequence (MSN560 → MSN561 → MSN562 → MSN563).
Minimum 500 supervised clinical hours required across PMHNP practicum courses.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required to remain in good standing.
Applicants must hold an active RN license and a BSN from a CCNE- or ACEN-accredited program.
notes: Graduates eligible to sit for ANCC Psychiatric-Mental Health NP certification examination.

core_required.description: MSN core courses required for all specializations (18 credits)

electives.description: Choose two graduate nursing electives (6 credits)

constraints: Total credits: 36 (core + specialization + electives).
MSN503 (Nursing Theory) must be completed before MSN570.
MSN570 → MSN571 → MSN572 must be taken in sequence.
MSN572 (Nurse Educator Practicum) requires 150 supervised educational practicum hours.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required to remain in good standing.
Applicants must hold an active RN license and a BSN from a CCNE- or ACEN-accredited program.
notes: Graduates eligible to sit for the NLN Certified Nurse Educator (CNE) examination.

core_required.description: MSN core courses required for all specializations (18 credits)

electives.description: Choose two graduate nursing electives (6 credits)

constraints: Total credits: 36 (core + specialization + electives).
MSN580 (Nursing Administration) must be completed before MSN581 and MSN582.
MSN580 → MSN581 → MSN582 must be taken in sequence.
MSN582 (Nurse Administrator Practicum) requires 150 supervised administrative practicum hours.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required to remain in good standing.
Applicants must hold an active RN license and a BSN from a CCNE- or ACEN-accredited program.
notes: Graduates eligible to sit for the AONE Nurse Manager and Leader (CNML) or ANCC Nurse Executive (NE-BC) certification examination.
